input.background: Assume \(\hat{\theta}_1\) and \(\hat{\theta}_2\) are the two estimators of parameter \(\theta\). Suppose that \(E(\hat{\theta}_1)=0.9\theta\), \(E(\hat{\theta}_2)=\theta\), \(\operatorname{var}(\hat{\theta}_1)=2\), and \(\operatorname{var}(\hat{\theta}_2)=3\).
input.question: What inequality can be used to establish a lower bound on the variance of an estimator?

output.answer: We will later discuss how to establish a lower bound on the variance using an inequality known as the Cramér-Rao inequality.
